name: parse_empty_is_none
reason: Parsing an empty sequence should fail — if it succeeded, the parser would produce values from no input

name: parse_single_byte_value_range
code:
proof fn phi_2_parse_single_byte_value_range(b: u8)
    requires
        b < 0x80,
    ensures
        UnsignedLEB128.spec_parse(seq![b]) == Some((1int, (b & 0x7f) as UInt)),
{
}
reason: A single byte below 0x80 should parse to its low 7 bits — if the base case produced a different value, all multi-byte encodings would be wrong

name: parse_single_continuation_byte_fails
code:
proof fn phi_3_parse_single_continuation_byte_fails(b: u8)
    requires
        b >= 0x80,
    ensures
        UnsignedLEB128.spec_parse(seq![b]).is_none(),
{
}
reason: A single continuation byte (high bit set) with no following data should fail to parse — if it succeeded, truncated encodings would silently produce incorrect values

name: parse_rejects_non_canonical_zero_continuation
reason: The encoding 0x80 0x00 is a non-canonical representation of 0 (v2 == 0 fails the `0 < v2` check) — if accepted, the same value would have multiple encodings, breaking injectivity

name: parse_consumed_at_most_input_length
reason: The number of bytes consumed should never exceed input length — if it did, the parser would claim to read past the end of the buffer
